original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: British Society for Allergy and Clinical Immunology
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United Kingdom specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, ENT, prescribing, and referral adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Children and adults with suspected or established allergic, non-allergic, occupational, or other rhinitis phenotypes, subject to section-specific qualifiers.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary, allergy, respiratory, paediatric, and ENT assessment and review.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for rhinitis history, triggers, nasal and eye symptoms, sleep and mouth-breathing impact, medicine and social context, nasal examination, septum, secretions, crusting, polyps, postnasal drip, and clinician-selected testing context. It does not diagnose a subtype or generate treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 7.1–7.10 — history, triggers, symptom pattern, impact, and contextual factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 8.1–8.3 — visual, nasal, oral, and upper-airway examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 9.1–9.8 — allergy testing and investigation context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.

title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)
publisher: British Society for Allergy and Clinical Immunology
jurisdiction: United Kingdom specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, ENT, prescribing, and referral adaptation
population: Children and adults with suspected or established allergic, non-allergic, occupational, or other rhinitis phenotypes, subject to section-specific qualifiers.
intended_setting: Primary, allergy, respiratory, paediatric, and ENT assessment and review.
access_limitations: One or more PDF tables require structured extraction
Algorithm or figure references preserved as locators; diagram structure not interpreted
